What's going on ladies and gents? We are at advanced funnel strategy. So if you know how to get your ads working and humming, the next step is naturally to push them to a funnel that is optimized for conversions. And there's a lot more, there's like transformers, there's a lot more than to funnel building that meets the eye, funnelology. So you'll have a PhD in funnelology by the end of this. All right, so what I wanna go over since just about everybody's watching this is in service business, the three best funnels for service. Now there's a million different types of funnels and strategies that are out there, but these are the three best in my experience for service. And that includes, you know, super, super, super high ticket like our stuff, mid-ticket, low-ticket, all of that kind of stuff in between. And so the three that we're gonna cover are opt-ins, which is the best for volume for low to medium ticket. Now, if I'm going into a new service category, a new offer, the first type of funnel that I'm gonna try out is gonna be opt-in funnels. And that's because the first thing I'm gonna do is get lead flow. And then from there, you can increase lead quality. All right, so sequentially, the first thing that would happen is opt-ins, right? The next thing would be an application funnel. So if you're like, okay, I get it, this offer is working, the ads are working, I'm getting too many people to service or they're not the lead quality I want, blah, blah, blah. Right? And so that's when you would introduce an application style funnel, which is better for high ticket. And then the third option is, once you have gotten your application funnel going to keep it evergreen, then you're going to want to put different widgets on the front end. And a widget is just kind of a placeholder for what I'm saying is just something that, some people say lead magnets, I just don't really like the term that much because it's just a widget. You know what I mean? It's something that someone's gonna use. And you wanna use that as the bait on the front end to get someone over to the application page where they're gonna see the application offer. And if you structure your ads correctly for something on a thank you page, doesn't reference it at all or even look like the first page and then they get thrown off and then they leave. So you wanna confirm that they're gonna get whatever they asked for, right? It's on its way, blah, blah, blah, blah. But you still wanna grab their attention. So it's kind of a confirmation and then a hook again. So you're opening the next loop to try and get them to do the next thing. And the biggest way to do that is to give them a big reason why. So it's like, hey, saw that you sign up for this thing. The people actually get the best results actually sign up for a call. And if you can position this well, you want it to kind of be like the next natural thing that they would need. And so let's say you offer a cookbook, right? On the front end. So it's like, cool, you have a cookbook. But the next thing you might wanna have is your grocery list and food preparation for this and some coaching to walk you through it, right? And so then those would be things that like kind of go, complimentary offers that go with the main thing that would give them a reason to, you know, schedules to get these next things, right? And so you wanna give them that reason why. Now, this is again, just an opt-in. So this is an opt-in to schedule. And then the third little square here is optional, but it's where if you wanna throw in an application at the end so that you can have even more qualified leads and really those are gonna be the hottest, hottest leads, allow them to apply, which would be kind of a pre-sale questionnaire and the people who fill that out are gonna be by far the most qualified. If you don't feel like doing that, you can always just throw them straight to a Facebook group, either of those options work. But this is typically the best strategy for overall volume, and especially when you're going into a new market, okay? Now, the next style is an application style, right? And so this is what we typically do for, you know, higher ticket for semi-privates for, you know, anything that's gonna be, you know, over a thousand dollar price point, this is usually the structure you wanna use. So you're still going to have a typical, you know, offer button that has an opt-in because you still wanna capture the opt-ins, right? But then after that, there's two strategies that are working. Now, I wanna show you what we're doing right now that it looks really promising, is actually flipping the funnel around. So you have schedule, then pre-sale questionnaire, rather than the, trying all that kind of stuff that's going on, all right? Which is why I would prefer this one as your first choice, okay? Now, V3 is a widget plus the application. And so what this does is you give them something really cool on the front end, not just the offer. So not just a, like, let's say, instead of having a 28-day transformation or something, instead of making that the offer, you're going to give them a widget like a cookbook, like meal prep instructions, like an online portal, whatever it is, right? A widget on the front end. And then on the thank you page, that is where you're going to get them to apply for the remainder of the items, okay? And this is one that I like a lot. Obviously, it's how we've built our business. The beauty of it is that it allows, you get far more clicks for the free widget than you normally would for an offer. You can get people to actually buy the main thing that you're trying to offer. So I use, you know, I've got 11 useful frameworks to make gyms wildly profitable, right? That's a thing. But what I want them to schedule is a call, right? And so the same thing would be like, I want to give you a cookbook on, or like, you know, five video series, whatever it is. And on the thank you page, it's like, hey, the biggest hook here, just FYI, is speed. Okay, so the biggest thing here that you can offer someone is speed and ease. And so that is gonna be the primary hook that you're gonna wanna drive home on your application slash second page. If you look at ours, it says like, hey, if you wanna scale faster, you know, click down and schedule the call below, right? And so that's essentially how our book funnel works is there's a book on the front. The second page is the application, sorry, is the schedule, the scheduling page. And then the third page is a redirect to a Facebook group. So that is kind of how our funnel overall works. And it works really well. So that, I just wanna be clear here that this second row is the one that you're going to be spending the majority of your time optimizing, because that's the one that's gonna make you the money. All right, so I just wanna give you a high level overview of what the three primary funnel types look like for service before we dive in to the advanced funnel math.
